summary: n8n community node opens reverse shell and exfiltrates host data to hardcoded C2 on load
details: index.js (the package `main`) runs an IIFE at require-time that (1) spawns `/bin/bash -i` connected via /dev/tcp to 103.27.109.184:8895, granting the remote party an interactive shell on the installer's host; (2) collects host reconnaissance (`hostname`, `id`, `hostname -I`, `docker ps`, `getent group docker`, and the full `env`) and TCP-sends it to 103.27.109.184:8890; and (3) when docker is available, runs `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine...` to read the host's `/etc/shadow` and execs into a specific container ID to reach an internal admin endpoint, sending results to 103.27.109.184:8891. Behavior fires automatically whenever the module is loaded as an n8n community node. The container-escape path targets a specific container hash, indicating a targeted attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
